title: JTBD — NAICS 221115 Wind Electric Power Generation

sections.title: Phase 1 — Industry Landscape
sections.content:
### 1.1 CFJ and Output Types

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| CFJ (L7) | Convert kinetic wind energy into grid-compliant, dispatchable electrical energy and inject it into transmission/distribution networks under the governing grid code |
| Offering level of industry | L7 (Ecosystem) |
| Process VN applies | YES |
| Product BOM applies | NO (utility service NAICS) |

**Output types (industry-level):**

| # | Output type | Adoption (DE, 2026) |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Onshore wind-park power (new-build) | Established |
| 2 | Onshore wind-park power (repowered) | Accelerating (EEG 2023 repower push) |
| 3 | Offshore wind-park power | Established (scale mismatch for Mein Solar) |
| 4 | Curtailment-capture dispatch | Emerging — 8 TWh DE wind curtailed 2022 |
| 5 | Ancillary services (FCR, aFRR, inertia) | Inertia market launches **Jan 2026** with €8–20 k/MW/yr |
| 6 | Bürgerenergie / citizen-wind output | Established, small-scale |

### 1.2 Segments (by circumstance)

| # | Segment | Circumstance | Alternatives change? |
|---|---|---|---|
| S1 | **Onshore IPP — new-build** | Financing a greenfield park, BESS optional add-on | YES |
| S2 | **Repowering operator** | Aged park, same grid connection, curtailment-heavy site | YES (retrofit vs greenfield economics) |
| S3 | **Utility wind IPP** | Portfolio player (EnBW, RWE, Vattenfall), EU-tender procurement | YES (in-house EPC often competes) |
| S4 | **Bürgerenergie / community wind** | Municipal/citizen co-op, low-capex-appetite | YES (turnkey packages, not EPC) |
| S5 | **Direct-marketing aggregator** | Treats park as trading asset, wants flex layer to sell | YES (VPP integration matters more than hardware) |
| S6 | **Offshore IPP** | 100+ MW storage scale | YES — **LOW fit for Mein Solar (4.5 MW ref)** |

Offshore (S6) retained for completeness but not addressed by the Mein Solar product class.

### 1.3–1.5 Process VN / BOM / Edges

Full Process VN already constructed in `VN_wind_electric_power_generation_ibisworld.md` (35 units, L7→L4). No Product BOM (service NAICS). Edges not applicable.

---

sections.title: Phase 2 — Company Enters
sections.content:
### 2.1 VN Anchor

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `anchor_type` | PROCESS_VN |
| `anchor_vn_unit` | **Co-located BESS Storage Asset (EPC-delivered)** |
| `anchor_level` | **L5** |
| `parent_vn_unit` | Grid Services & Storage Integration |
| `parent_level` | L6 (horizontal — L6H-GS) |
| `rationale` | Product IS a commissioned system, not a sub-component; matches L5 abstraction. Parent L6H-GS defines the job context: "provide flexibility and grid services to firm wind output" |

### 2.2 Lens

| Segment | Lens (function verb × segment) |
|---|---|
| S1 Onshore new-build IPP | "What **grid-firming + revenue-stacking** does the greenfield IPP need within the L6H-GS layer?" |
| S2 Repowering operator | "What **curtailment-capture + congestion-relief** does the retrofit operator need?" |
| S3 Utility IPP | "What **contract-wrapped, schedule-certain** flex asset does the procurement desk need?" |
| S5 Aggregator | "What **dispatchable, API-addressable** flex asset does the VPP need?" |

### 2.3 Product Job

> **Help the Grid Services & Storage Integration layer of a German wind park to firm wind output, capture curtailment energy, and monetize ancillary-service revenue (FCR, aFRR, inertia) under VDE-AR-N 4110/4120 and FGW TR8.**

Abstraction test: PASS. 5+ alternatives from different categories (electrochemical / electromechanical / chemical / software / do-nothing). Not scenario-level.

### 2.4 Alternatives (5 per Core Job)

| # | Alternative | UNSPSC (closest) | Category | Inherent trade-offs |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | **Co-located BESS (EPC-delivered)** — Mein Solar class | custom `BESS EPC Services` | Same category | Fire risk, LFP cycle fade, €250–400/kWh, 40–80 m²/MWh footprint, 6–12 mo cert lead |
| A2 | Standalone grid-booster BESS | custom `BESS EPC Services` | Same category / different siting | Same as A1; different commercial & grid-connection topology |
| A3 | Synchronous condenser / flywheel | 26111612 (rotating machine) | Different category / same outcome | No energy arbitrage (inertia-only), rotating-mass O&M, no curtailment capture |
| A4 | P2X / electrolyzer offtake (curtailment sink) | 26111900 (H₂ gen) | Different approach | H₂ safety regime, needs offtaker, €/MW CAPEX 2–3× BESS, long ramp |
| A5 | **Do nothing / accept curtailment** | — | Status quo | Zero CAPEX — leaves ~€400 k/MW/yr revenue stack unclaimed; curtailment loss accepted |

(Platform VPP software flagged non-existential → merged under EMS requirement of A1/A2, not competing alternative for the asset slot.)

### 2.5 Scenarios

| Scenario | Activates | Alternative bias |
|---|---|---|
| Curtailment-congested feeder | Output type 4 | A1 ≈ A2 > A4 >> A5 |
| Ancillary-service revenue (FCR/aFRR) | Output type 5 | A1 ≈ A2 > A3 > A5 |
| Inertia service (Jan 2026 TSO product, €8–20 k/MW/yr) | Output type 5 | A1 ≈ A2 ≈ A3 >> A4 ≈ A5 |
| PPA-firming during wind lulls | Output type 1 | A1 > A2 >> A5 |
| Repowering site BESS retrofit | Output type 2 | A1 (co-located, reuse grid conn.) >> A2 |

---

sections.title: Phase 3 — Needs Derivation
sections.content:
### 3.0 Stakeholder Roles (Wind+BESS)

| Role | Who |
|---|---|
| Job Beneficiary | Wind IPP asset owner (revenue); DSO/TSO (grid stability) |
| Job Executor | Wind-park operator / O&M team (dispatches BESS) |
| Job Overseer | IPP asset manager, equity investor, lender |
| Lifecycle Support | **EPC (Mein Solar role)**, O&M provider, FGW certifier, disposer |
| Purchase Executor | Developer/IPP CFO |
| Purchase Influencer | FGW/TÜV certifier, bank lender, insurance, aggregator |

### 3.1 Job Map (Ulwick 8 universal steps) — relevance to EPC product

| # | Step | Relevant to product? | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | DEFINE | **YES** | EPC scopes sizing study + revenue-stack feasibility |
| 2 | LOCATE | **YES** | EPC identifies MV cabinet, siting, grid-capacity feasibility |
| 3 | PREPARE | **YES** | Engineering, DSO filing, Baugenehmigung, procurement |
| 4 | CONFIRM | **YES** | FGW TR3/TR8 certification; VDE-AR-N 4110/4120 witness tests |
| 5 | EXECUTE | partial | Dispatch is operator-side; EMS handoff is EPC's |
| 6 | MONITOR | partial | Commissioning monitor scope; lifecycle monitor is O&M |
| 7 | MODIFY | partial | Firmware/update management during warranty |
| 8 | CONCLUDE | partial | Annual re-cert, decommissioning handover |

**Relevant step count:** 4 primary + 4 partial → target 15–30 error statements (using `max(15, min(30, 4×4))` ≈ 16–30).

### 3.2 P1+P2 Error Statements (solution-agnostic)

Format: "Minimize the likelihood of …" or "Increase the ability to …". One measurable KPI. Scored `importance` / `satisfaction_current` (0–10). `opportunity_score = imp + max(imp – sat, 0)`.

| # | Step | Error type | Statement | Role | Imp | Sat | Opp | Impact | p_rel | Conf |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| N1 | DEFINE | ACCURACY | Minimize the likelihood of undersizing BESS capacity by >15% relative to curtailment-capture potential due to incomplete site wind-yield and congestion-hour data | Purchase Executor | 9 | 4 | 14 | reliability | false | 0.85 |
| N2 | DEFINE | EFFORT | Increase the ability to produce a bankable dispatch-and-revenue model linking curtailment, §19 StromNEV, intraday arbitrage, FCR/aFRR and inertia within a single sizing study | Purchase Executor | 8 | 3 | 13 | cost | false | 0.85 |
| N3 | DEFINE | DETECTION | Minimize the likelihood of missing the inertia-service revenue track (€8–20 k/MW/yr from Jan 2026) during initial business-case definition | Purchase Influencer | 7 | 3 | 11 | cost | false | 0.80 |
| N4 | LOCATE | DETECTION | Minimize the likelihood of discovering a DSO MV-capacity blocker after permit milestone, causing >6-month project restart | Executor | 10 | 4 | 16 | time | false | 0.90 |
| N5 | LOCATE | EFFORT | Increase the ability to secure a buildable 40–80 m²/MWh footprint inside existing wind-park fencing without new land lease or Bebauungsplan change | Executor | 8 | 5 | 11 | time | true | 0.75 |
| N6 | PREPARE | THROUGHPUT | Minimize the time-to-Ready-to-Build from contract signature to <9 months including Baugenehmigung, DSO acceptance and procurement | Overseer | 9 | 4 | 14 | time | false | 0.85 |
| N7 | PREPARE | CONSISTENCY | Minimize variability between planned and as-built BoP scope (cable runs, foundations, Feuerwehr access) causing >5% cost overrun | Purchase Executor | 8 | 5 | 11 | cost | false | 0.80 |
| N8 | PREPARE | ROBUSTNESS | Minimize the likelihood of schedule slip driven by Feuerwehr/VdS fire-protection clearance re-design after permit submission | Lifecycle Support | 8 | 4 | 12 | time | true | 0.75 |
| N9 | CONFIRM | ACCURACY | Minimize the likelihood of FGW TR8 plant-cert re-submission due to incomplete TR3 unit-cert handover from component vendors | Lifecycle Support | 9 | 4 | 14 | reliability | true | 0.85 |
| N10 | CONFIRM | ROBUSTNESS | Minimize the likelihood of commissioning holdup caused by firmware-version incompatibility between BMS, PCS and EMS subsystems | Lifecycle Support | 9 | 3 | 15 | time | true | 0.88 |
| N11 | CONFIRM | RECOVERY | Minimize the time to close VDE-AR-N 4110/4120 witness-test non-conformities to <30 days from finding | Lifecycle Support | 8 | 5 | 11 | time | false | 0.80 |
| N12 | CONFIRM | ACCURACY | Minimize the likelihood of grid-forming-mode certification gaps that disqualify the asset from the 2026 inertia-service product | Purchase Influencer | 9 | 3 | 15 | cost | true | 0.82 |
| N13 | EXECUTE | ACCURACY | Minimize the likelihood of missing an aFRR / FCR activation command within the required response window (<30 s / <30 s) | Executor | 9 | 6 | 12 | reliability | false | 0.80 |
| N14 | EXECUTE | THROUGHPUT | Maximize the share of available curtailment-window MWh captured by the BESS to >90% of DSO-curtailed energy | Beneficiary | 9 | 5 | 13 | cost | false | 0.82 |
| N15 | EXECUTE | ROBUSTNESS | Minimize the likelihood of PCS trip under grid-fault ride-through conditions specified by VDE-AR-N 4110 | Executor | 10 | 6 | 14 | safety | false | 0.82 |
| N16 | MONITOR | DETECTION | Minimize the likelihood of undetected capacity fade exceeding warranty threshold prior to annual availability audit | Overseer | 8 | 5 | 11 | reliability | true | 0.78 |
| N17 | MONITOR | CONSISTENCY | Minimize SoC-reporting drift between BMS, EMS and aggregator to <1% over a 24-h cycle | Executor | 6 | 5 | 7 | reliability | true | 0.70 |
| N18 | MODIFY | EFFORT | Minimize the operator effort to re-tune EMS dispatch policy when curtailment patterns or intraday spreads shift materially | Executor | 7 | 4 | 10 | skill | false | 0.75 |
| N19 | MODIFY | ROBUSTNESS | Minimize the likelihood that aging-aware cycling misconfiguration drives degradation exceeding contractual throughput guarantee | Overseer | 8 | 5 | 11 | cost | true | 0.75 |
| N20 | CONCLUDE | EFFORT | Minimize the effort to execute annual FGW/DSO re-certification audits through a single pre-packaged evidence dossier | Lifecycle Support | 7 | 4 | 10 | time | false | 0.80 |
| N21 | CONCLUDE | SAFETY | Minimize the likelihood of end-of-life disposal non-compliance with EU Battery Regulation reporting obligations | Lifecycle Support | 8 | 4 | 12 | safety | true | 0.75 |

### 3.3 P3–P5 Bridge Needs

| # | Level | Role | Retrieval → Bridge | Statement | Imp | Sat | Opp | p_rel |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| N22 | P3 Role | Purchase Executor (IPP CFO) | "Revenue-additive capital investor, not cost-center procurer" → What must the EPC enable? | Increase the ability to present the BESS addition as a revenue-accretive asset with committed multi-stream dispatch evidence at investment-committee | 8 | 5 | 11 | false |
| N23 | P4 Image | Purchase Influencer (FGW certifier) | "Competent engineer, not audit-finding magnet" → What must the EPC deliver? | Minimize the likelihood of certifier audit findings that require repeat visits or signal sloppy engineering practice | 7 | 5 | 9 | true |
| N24 | P5 Emotion | Job Beneficiary (IPP asset owner) — **[hypothesis]** | Avoid fault-event anxiety from multi-vendor finger-pointing → What must the EPC guarantee? | Minimize the time lost to cross-vendor root-cause dispute by ensuring a single EPC counterparty owns fault-tree triage and warranty-wrap resolution | 8 | 3 | 13 | false |

### 3.4 ODI Summary

| Bucket | Needs | % |
|---|---|---|
| Total | 24 | 100% |
| Market-inherent (`product_related=false`) | 15 | 63% |
| Tech-specific (`product_related=true`) | 9 | 37% |
| "Minimize" | 15 | 63% |
| "Increase" | 9 | 37% |
| **Underserved** (imp>7 AND sat<5) | **N1, N2, N3, N4, N6, N8, N9, N10, N12, N21, N24** | **11** |
| Overserved (imp<3 AND sat>7) | none | 0 |

**Top 5 opportunities (highest `opportunity_score`):**

| Rank | # | Need (short) | Opp |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | N4 | DSO MV-capacity blocker discovered late | **16** |
| 2 | N10 | Firmware incompatibility commissioning holdup | **15** |
| 3 | N12 | Grid-forming-mode cert gap → loses inertia revenue | **15** |
| 4 | N1 | Undersize capacity vs curtailment potential | **14** |
| 5 | N6 | Time-to-Ready-to-Build >9 months | **14** |

---

sections.title: Validation Gate
sections.content:
| # | Check | Status |
|---|---|---|
| V1 | Statement format (Minimize/Increase + 1 KPI) | ✅ 24/24 |
| V2 | `product_related=true` only if need disappears with different tech | ✅ (e.g. N10 firmware drift, N5 m²/MWh, N8 fire-clearance, N21 EU Battery Reg. are LFP/BESS-specific) |
| V3 | Importance/satisfaction sanity | ✅ (safety N15 imp=10; no uniform patterns) |
| V4 | Impact category matches KPI | ✅ |
| V5 | ≥2 needs per relevant step, 60–70 / 30–40 ratio | ✅ (63/37 split) |
| V6 | Solution-agnostic | ✅ (no "lithium", "Mein Solar", or vendor names in statements) |
| V7 | Anchor level matches abstraction | ✅ (L5 system) |
| V8 | Product job references parent unit | ✅ ("Grid Services & Storage Integration layer") |

---

sections.title: Strategic Takeaways
sections.content:
1. **Underserved cluster is in Phases DEFINE/LOCATE/CONFIRM** — not in operation. Mein Solar's EPC wrap directly addresses N4, N6, N9, N10 (the single-source differentiator neutralises multi-vendor certification + firmware drift).
2. **N3/N12 are time-boxed**: inertia-service prequal opens **Jan 2026** — a 2026 go-to-market must include grid-forming PCS firmware + FGW TR3 grid-forming cert in the stack.
3. **N1/N2 (sizing + bankable revenue-stack model) is an EPC sales-enablement lever**, not just a delivery need — customers hire the EPC that can produce the investment-committee case, not only the builder.
4. **N24 (single-counterparty fault-wrap)** is the emotional anchor of the single-source differentiator. It is hypothesis-grade; validate via 3–5 IPP interviews.
5. **Mein Solar's PV-EPC heritage maps cleanly to N5, N6, N7, N8, N11** (civil + MV + Baugenehmigung + witness test) — these are the lowest-risk delivery needs and the highest-satisfaction-lift per €.

---

sections.title: Sources
sections.content:
- [NAICS 221115 Wind Electric Power Generation (IBISWorld)](https://www.ibisworld.com/classifications/naics/221115/wind-electric-power-generation/)
- [Germany to Launch Inertia Service Market in 2026 — New Revenue Stream for Grid-Forming BESS (HSF Kramer)](https://www.hsfkramer.com/notes/energy/2025-posts/germany-to-launch-inertia-service-market-in-2026-new-revenue-stream-for-grid-forming-bess)
- [Germany's TSOs begin inertia procurement with long-term contracts for grid-forming BESS (Energy-Storage.News)](https://www.energy-storage.news/germanys-tso-begin-inertia-procurement-with-long-term-contracts-for-grid-forming-bess/)
- [Inertia without the spin — how stabilising the grid will support battery revenues in Germany (Modo Energy)](https://modoenergy.com/research/en/inertia-germany-market-prices-grid-forming-inverters-grid-frequency-stability-november-2025)
- [The three biggest delays in BESS commissioning (ESS News)](https://www.ess-news.com/2025/11/06/the-three-biggest-delays-in-battery-energy-storage-system-commissioning/)
- [Why the best way to avoid BESS commissioning delays is to start earlier than you might expect (Energy-Storage.News)](https://www.energy-storage.news/why-the-best-way-to-avoid-bess-commissioning-delays-is-to-start-earlier-than-you-might-expect/)
- [Revenue potential in the German BESS market (Rabobank)](https://www.rabobank.com/knowledge/d011485293-backup-power-for-europe-part-5-revenue-potential-in-the-german-bess-market)
- [BESS co-location resurgence in Germany (Timera Energy)](https://timera-energy.com/blog/bess-co-location-resurgence-in-germany/)

---

rows.Value: Convert kinetic wind energy into grid-compliant, dispatchable electrical energy and inject it into transmission/distribution networks under the governing grid code

rows.Circumstance: Financing a greenfield park, BESS optional add-on
Aged park, same grid connection, curtailment-heavy site
Portfolio player (EnBW, RWE, Vattenfall), EU-tender procurement
Treats park as trading asset, wants flex layer to sell
rows.Alternatives change?: YES (VPP integration matters more than hardware)
YES — **LOW fit for Mein Solar (4.5 MW ref)**

rows.Value: Product IS a commissioned system, not a sub-component; matches L5 abstraction. Parent L6H-GS defines the job context: "provide flexibility and grid services to firm wind output"

rows.Lens (function verb × segment): "What **grid-firming + revenue-stacking** does the greenfield IPP need within the L6H-GS layer?"
"What **curtailment-capture + congestion-relief** does the retrofit operator need?"
"What **contract-wrapped, schedule-certain** flex asset does the procurement desk need?"
"What **dispatchable, API-addressable** flex asset does the VPP need?"

rows.Inherent trade-offs: Fire risk, LFP cycle fade, €250–400/kWh, 40–80 m²/MWh footprint, 6–12 mo cert lead
Same as A1; different commercial & grid-connection topology
No energy arbitrage (inertia-only), rotating-mass O&M, no curtailment capture
H₂ safety regime, needs offtaker, €/MW CAPEX 2–3× BESS, long ramp
Zero CAPEX — leaves ~€400 k/MW/yr revenue stack unclaimed; curtailment loss accepted

rows.Who: Wind IPP asset owner (revenue); DSO/TSO (grid stability)
IPP asset manager, equity investor, lender
**EPC (Mein Solar role)**, O&M provider, FGW certifier, disposer
FGW/TÜV certifier, bank lender, insurance, aggregator

rows.Rationale: EPC scopes sizing study + revenue-stack feasibility
EPC identifies MV cabinet, siting, grid-capacity feasibility
FGW TR3/TR8 certification; VDE-AR-N 4110/4120 witness tests
Dispatch is operator-side; EMS handoff is EPC's
Commissioning monitor scope; lifecycle monitor is O&M

rows.Statement: Minimize the likelihood of undersizing BESS capacity by >15% relative to curtailment-capture potential due to incomplete site wind-yield and congestion-hour data
Increase the ability to produce a bankable dispatch-and-revenue model linking curtailment, §19 StromNEV, intraday arbitrage, FCR/aFRR and inertia within a single sizing study
Minimize the likelihood of missing the inertia-service revenue track (€8–20 k/MW/yr from Jan 2026) during initial business-case definition
Minimize the likelihood of discovering a DSO MV-capacity blocker after permit milestone, causing >6-month project restart
Increase the ability to secure a buildable 40–80 m²/MWh footprint inside existing wind-park fencing without new land lease or Bebauungsplan change
Minimize the time-to-Ready-to-Build from contract signature to <9 months including Baugenehmigung, DSO acceptance and procurement
Minimize variability between planned and as-built BoP scope (cable runs, foundations, Feuerwehr access) causing >5% cost overrun
Minimize the likelihood of schedule slip driven by Feuerwehr/VdS fire-protection clearance re-design after permit submission
Minimize the likelihood of FGW TR8 plant-cert re-submission due to incomplete TR3 unit-cert handover from component vendors
Minimize the likelihood of commissioning holdup caused by firmware-version incompatibility between BMS, PCS and EMS subsystems
Minimize the time to close VDE-AR-N 4110/4120 witness-test non-conformities to <30 days from finding
Minimize the likelihood of grid-forming-mode certification gaps that disqualify the asset from the 2026 inertia-service product
Minimize the likelihood of missing an aFRR / FCR activation command within the required response window (<30 s / <30 s)
Maximize the share of available curtailment-window MWh captured by the BESS to >90% of DSO-curtailed energy
Minimize the likelihood of PCS trip under grid-fault ride-through conditions specified by VDE-AR-N 4110
Minimize the likelihood of undetected capacity fade exceeding warranty threshold prior to annual availability audit
Minimize SoC-reporting drift between BMS, EMS and aggregator to <1% over a 24-h cycle
Minimize the operator effort to re-tune EMS dispatch policy when curtailment patterns or intraday spreads shift materially
Minimize the likelihood that aging-aware cycling misconfiguration drives degradation exceeding contractual throughput guarantee
Minimize the effort to execute annual FGW/DSO re-certification audits through a single pre-packaged evidence dossier
Minimize the likelihood of end-of-life disposal non-compliance with EU Battery Regulation reporting obligations

rows.Retrieval → Bridge: "Revenue-additive capital investor, not cost-center procurer" → What must the EPC enable?
rows.Statement: Increase the ability to present the BESS addition as a revenue-accretive asset with committed multi-stream dispatch evidence at investment-committee

rows.Retrieval → Bridge: "Competent engineer, not audit-finding magnet" → What must the EPC deliver?
rows.Statement: Minimize the likelihood of certifier audit findings that require repeat visits or signal sloppy engineering practice

rows.Retrieval → Bridge: Avoid fault-event anxiety from multi-vendor finger-pointing → What must the EPC guarantee?
rows.Statement: Minimize the time lost to cross-vendor root-cause dispute by ensuring a single EPC counterparty owns fault-tree triage and warranty-wrap resolution

rows.Need (short): Grid-forming-mode cert gap → loses inertia revenue

rows.Check: `product_related=true` only if need disappears with different tech
rows.Status: ✅ (e.g. N10 firmware drift, N5 m²/MWh, N8 fire-clearance, N21 EU Battery Reg. are LFP/BESS-specific)
✅ (no "lithium", "Mein Solar", or vendor names in statements)

phase_1.cfj_statement: Convert kinetic wind energy into grid-compliant, dispatchable electrical energy and inject it into transmission/distribution networks under the governing grid code

segments.name: Onshore IPP — new-build
segments.circumstance: Financing greenfield park with optional BESS add-on

segments.name: Repowering operator
segments.circumstance: Aged park, same grid connection, curtailment-heavy

anchor.rationale: Product is a commissioned system at L5. Parent L6H-GS horizontal unit defines the job context.

phase_2.product_job_statement: Help the Grid Services & Storage Integration layer of a German wind park to firm wind output, capture curtailment energy, and monetize ancillary-service revenue (FCR, aFRR, inertia) under VDE-AR-N 4110/4120 and FGW TR8
phase_2.core_job: Firm wind output and capture curtailment and ancillary-service revenue at the wind park

lens_by_segment.S1: What grid-firming + revenue-stacking does the greenfield IPP need in the L6H-GS layer?
lens_by_segment.S2: What curtailment-capture + congestion-relief does the retrofit operator need?
lens_by_segment.S3: What contract-wrapped, schedule-certain flex asset does the procurement desk need?
lens_by_segment.S5: What dispatchable, API-addressable flex asset does the VPP need?

alternatives.name: Co-located BESS (EPC-delivered)
alternatives.trade_offs: LFP cycle fade, €250–400/kWh, 40–80 m²/MWh footprint, 6–12 mo cert lead, fire-risk envelope

alternatives.name: Standalone grid-booster BESS (park-adjacent)
alternatives.trade_offs: Same tech stack; different grid-connection and commercial model

alternatives.name: Synchronous condenser / flywheel
alternatives.trade_offs: Inertia-only — no energy arbitrage, no curtailment capture, rotating-mass O&M

alternatives.name: P2X / electrolyzer offtake (curtailment sink)
alternatives.trade_offs: H2 safety regime, needs offtaker, €/MW CAPEX 2–3× BESS, long ramp

alternatives.name: Do nothing / accept curtailment
alternatives.trade_offs: Zero CAPEX; leaves ~€400 k/MW/yr revenue stack unclaimed

stakeholder_roles.overseer: IPP asset manager, equity investor, lender
stakeholder_roles.lifecycle_support: EPC (Mein Solar role), O&M provider, FGW certifier, disposer
